verses.text: Open thy doors, O Lebanon; the shepherds howl, for their glory is spoiled. Coming ruin. The proud and their pastures alike are laid waste.
Open thy doors, O Lebanon; the shepherds howl, for their glory is spoiled. Coming ruin. The proud and their pastures alike are laid waste.
Open thy doors, O Lebanon; the shepherds howl, for their glory is spoiled. Coming ruin. The proud and their pastures alike are laid waste.
'Feed the flock of the slaughter,' whose owners slay them; God will no more pity the land's inhabitants. Doomed flock. A people who reject their shepherd are given up.
'Feed the flock of the slaughter,' whose owners slay them; God will no more pity the land's inhabitants. Doomed flock. A people who reject their shepherd are given up.
'Feed the flock of the slaughter,' whose owners slay them; God will no more pity the land's inhabitants. Doomed flock. A people who reject their shepherd are given up.
He fed the flock with two staves, Beauty and Bands; three shepherds he cut off, and his soul loathed them. Tender care rejected. The good Shepherd's grace is met with hatred.
He fed the flock with two staves, Beauty and Bands; three shepherds he cut off, and his soul loathed them. Tender care rejected. The good Shepherd's grace is met with hatred.
'I will not feed you'; He broke Beauty, breaking the covenant; the poor of the flock knew it was the LORD's word. Covenant severed. When men despise the Shepherd, He withdraws His protection.
'I will not feed you'; He broke Beauty, breaking the covenant; the poor of the flock knew it was the LORD's word. Covenant severed. When men despise the Shepherd, He withdraws His protection.
'I will not feed you'; He broke Beauty, breaking the covenant; the poor of the flock knew it was the LORD's word. Covenant severed. When men despise the Shepherd, He withdraws His protection.
He asked his price; 'they weighed for my price thirty pieces of silver'; the LORD said, 'Cast it unto the potter.' Betrayal foretold. Matthew sees this fulfilled in Judas's thirty pieces.
He asked his price; 'they weighed for my price thirty pieces of silver'; the LORD said, 'Cast it unto the potter.' Betrayal foretold. Matthew sees this fulfilled in Judas's thirty pieces.
Then He broke Bands, breaking the brotherhood between Judah and Israel. Division. Rejecting the Shepherd shatters even the bonds among the people.
Take a foolish shepherd's tools; woe to the worthless shepherd who deserts the flock; the sword shall blind and wither him. False shepherd judged. Those who abandon God's flock face His sword.
